DevOps
01 — About Me

Turning curiosity into infrastructure.

I'm Harsh, a self-taught DevOps engineer in the making. I believe the best way to learn is to build things, break things, and write about it honestly.

My goal is to become a DevOps & Cloud Engineer who truly understands the systems they manage — from the Linux kernel up to Kubernetes clusters and CI/CD pipelines.

Every day I study, I document. Every concept I crack, I share. That's the deal I made with myself when I started this journey.

💡 Quick Actions
📖 GitHub Pages Deployment Guide
1. Edit content using the sidebar tabs above.
2. Click "Save All Changes" — persists everything to your browser.
3. Click "↓ Download index.html" to get a file with all changes baked in.
4. Upload to GitHub repo yourusername.github.io as index.html.
5. Repo Settings → Pages → Deploy from branch → main. Done! 🚀
⚡ Pro tip: Set up GitHub Auto-Sync in Settings — it pushes index.html + every blog/project as a separate file on every Save. index.html stays tiny forever.

Settings
GitHub Auto-Sync, Resume URL, password and data management.
🔗 GitHub Auto-Sync
✓ Active
Set this up once — every "Save All Changes" automatically pushes your updated index.html to GitHub, plus each blog post and project as its own separate file (blogs/blog-0.html, projects/proj-0.html, etc). This keeps index.html tiny forever — no matter how many posts you add. Live in ~30s.
Stored in your browser only. Never sent anywhere except GitHub's API. Requires repo scope.
📄 Resume / CV
🔒 Change Password
📦 Data Management
↓ Download index.html — bakes all your edits into a standalone file. Replace the file on GitHub to publish permanently.
Add Project
Add image URLs or upload files
Upload a full HTML page — visitors see a "View Full Page" button that opens it in a reader, just like blog posts.
